- Anscombe, G. E. M. (1957/1963). *[Intention](https://www.hup.harvard.edu/books/9780674003996)*. Basil Blackwell, 1957; 2nd ed. Harvard University Press, 1963.
- Aristotle. *Nicomachean Ethics* (Book VI) and *De Motu Animalium*. (Multiple editions; see [MIT Classics Archive](http://classics.mit.edu/Aristotle/nicomachaen.html).)
- Bai, Y. et al. (2022). [Constitutional AI: Harmlessness from AI Feedback](https://arxiv.org/abs/2212.08073). arXiv:2212.08073.
- Belinkov, Y. (2022). [Probing Classifiers: Promises, Shortcomings, and Advances](https://arxiv.org/abs/2102.12452). *Computational Linguistics*, 48(1):207–219. arXiv:2102.12452.
- Bender, E. M., & Koller, A. (2020). [Climbing towards NLU: On Meaning, Form, and Understanding in the Age of Data](https://aclanthology.org/2020.acl-main.463/). *ACL 2020*. ACL Anthology.
- Bender, E. M., Gebru, T., McMillan-Major, A., & Shmitchell, S. (2021). [On the Dangers of Stochastic Parrots: Can Language Models Be Too Big?](https://doi.org/10.1145/3442188.3445922) *FAccT 2021*. ACM. DOI: 10.1145/3442188.3445922.
- Bratman, M. (1987). *[Intention, Plans, and Practical Reason](https://www.hup.harvard.edu/books/9780674458185)*. Harvard University Press.
- Bubeck, S. et al. (2023). [Sparks of Artificial General Intelligence: Early Experiments with GPT-4](https://arxiv.org/abs/2303.12712). arXiv:2303.12712.
- Butlin, P., Long, R., Schwitzgebel, E., Bengio, Y., et al. (2023). [Consciousness in Artificial Intelligence: Insights from the Science of Consciousness](https://arxiv.org/abs/2308.08708). arXiv:2308.08708.
- Chalmers, D. J. (1995). [Facing Up to the Problem of Consciousness](https://consc.net/papers/facing.html). *Journal of Consciousness Studies*, 2(3), 200–219.
- Chalmers, D. J. (2023). [Could a Large Language Model be Conscious?](https://arxiv.org/abs/2303.07103) arXiv:2303.07103. (NeurIPS 2022 keynote; published *Boston Review*, 2023.)
- Chalmers, D. J. (2025). [Propositional Interpretability in Artificial Intelligence](https://arxiv.org/abs/2501.15740). arXiv:2501.15740.
- Clark, A., & Chalmers, D. (1998). [The Extended Mind](https://doi.org/10.1093/analys/58.1.7). *Analysis*, 58(1), 7–19. DOI: 10.1093/analys/58.1.7.
- Clark, A. (2003). *Natural-Born Cyborgs*. Oxford University Press.
- Davidson, D. (1963). Actions, Reasons, and Causes. *Journal of Philosophy*, 60(23), 685–700. Reprinted in *[Essays on Actions and Events](https://doi.org/10.1093/0199246270.001.0001)* (Oxford, 1980).
- Dennett, D. C. (1987). *[The Intentional Stance](https://mitpress.mit.edu/9780262540537/the-intentional-stance/)*. MIT Press.
- Dreyfus, H. L. (1972). *[What Computers Can't Do: A Critique of Artificial Reason](https://archive.org/details/whatcomputerscan00dreyrich)*. Harper & Row.
- Dreyfus, H. L. (1992). *[What Computers Still Can't Do](https://mitpress.mit.edu/9780262540674/what-computers-still-cant-do/)*. MIT Press.
- Elhage, N. et al. (2022). [Toy Models of Superposition](https://transformer-circuits.pub/2022/toy_model/index.html). *Transformer Circuits Thread*, Anthropic.
- Elazar, Y., Kassner, N., Ravfogel, S., Ravichander, A., Hovy, E., Schütze, H., & Goldberg, Y. (2021). [Measuring and Improving Consistency in Pretrained Language Models](https://arxiv.org/abs/2102.01017). *TACL*, 9:1964–1981. arXiv:2102.01017.
- Fodor, J. A. (1975). *[The Language of Thought](https://www.hup.harvard.edu/books/9780674510302)*. Harvard University Press.
- Floridi, L., & Cowls, J. (2019). [A Unified Framework of Five Principles for AI in Society](https://doi.org/10.1162/99608f92.8cd550d1). *Harvard Data Science Review*, 1(1). DOI: 10.1162/99608f92.8cd550d1.
- Frankfurt, H. G. (1971). [Freedom of the Will and the Concept of a Person](https://www.pdcnet.org/jphil/content/jphil_1971_0068_0001_0005_0020). *Journal of Philosophy*, 68(1), 5–20.
- Gettier, E. L. (1963). [Is Justified True Belief Knowledge?](https://doi.org/10.2307/3326922) *Analysis*, 23(6), 121–123. DOI: 10.2307/3326922.
- Geva, M., Schuster, R., Berant, J., & Levy, O. (2021). [Transformer Feed-Forward Layers Are Key-Value Memories](https://arxiv.org/abs/2012.14913). *EMNLP 2021*. arXiv:2012.14913.
- Gibson, J. J. (1979). *[The Ecological Approach to Visual Perception](https://doi.org/10.4324/9781315740218)*. Houghton Mifflin. DOI: 10.4324/9781315740218.
- Goldstein, S., & Lederman, H. (manuscript). [What Does ChatGPT Want? An Interpretationist Guide](https://philpapers.org/rec/GOLWDC-2). PhilPapers.
- Grzankowski, A., Keeling, G., Shevlin, H., & Street, W. (2025). [Deflating Deflationism: A Critical Perspective on Debunking Arguments Against LLM Mentality](https://arxiv.org/abs/2506.13403). arXiv:2506.13403.
- Lederman, H., & Mahowald, K. (2024). [Are Language Models More Like Libraries or Like Librarians? Bibliotechnism, the Novel Reference Problem, and the Attitudes of LLMs](https://arxiv.org/abs/2401.04854). *Transactions of the Association for Computational Linguistics*. arXiv:2401.04854.
- Lederman, H., & Mahowald, K. (2025). [Dissociating Direct Access and Inference in AI Introspection](https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.05414). arXiv:2603.05414.
- Herrmann, D. A., & Levinstein, B. A. (2025). [Standards for Belief Representations in LLMs](https://arxiv.org/abs/2405.21030). *Minds and Machines*, 2025. arXiv:2405.21030.
- Huang, L., Yu, W., Ma, W., Zhong, W., Feng, Z., Wang, H., Chen, Q., Peng, W., Feng, X., Qin, B., & Liu, T. (2023). [A Survey on Hallucination in Large Language Models: Principles, Taxonomy, Challenges, and Open Questions](https://arxiv.org/abs/2311.05232). arXiv:2311.05232.
- Ji, Z., Lee, N., Frieske, R., Yu, T., Su, D., Xu, Y., Ishii, E., Bang, Y., Madotto, A., & Fung, P. (2023). [Survey of Hallucination in Natural Language Generation](https://arxiv.org/abs/2202.03629). *ACM Computing Surveys*, 55(12):1–38. arXiv:2202.03629.
- Juarrero, A. (1999). *[Dynamics in Action: Intentional Behavior as a Complex System](https://mitpress.mit.edu/9780262600477/dynamics-in-action/)*. MIT Press.
- Kadavath, S. et al. (2022). [Language Models (Mostly) Know What They Know](https://arxiv.org/abs/2207.05221). arXiv:2207.05221. Anthropic.
- Keeling, G., & Street, W. (2024). [On the Attribution of Confidence to Large Language Models](https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.08388). *Inquiry*, 2025. arXiv:2407.08388.
- LeCun, Y. (2022). [A Path Towards Autonomous Machine Intelligence](https://openreview.net/pdf?id=BZ5a1r-kVsf). OpenReview (v0.9.2).
- Lin, S., Hilton, J., & Evans, O. (2022). [TruthfulQA: Measuring How Models Mimic Human Falsehoods](https://arxiv.org/abs/2109.07958). *ACL 2022*. arXiv:2109.07958.
- Marks, S., & Tegmark, M. (2023). [The Geometry of Truth: Emergent Linear Structure in Large Language Model Representations of True/False Datasets](https://arxiv.org/abs/2310.06824). arXiv:2310.06824.
- Meng, K., Bau, D., Andonian, A., & Belinkov, Y. (2022). [Locating and Editing Factual Associations in GPT](https://arxiv.org/abs/2202.05262). *NeurIPS 2022*. arXiv:2202.05262. (ROME)
- Meng, K., Sharma, A. S., Andonian, A., Belinkov, Y., & Bau, D. (2022). [Mass-Editing Memory in a Transformer](https://arxiv.org/abs/2210.07229). *ICLR 2023*. arXiv:2210.07229. (MEMIT)
- Millière, R., & Buckner, C. (2024). [A Philosophical Introduction to Language Models — Part I: Continuity With Classic Debates](https://arxiv.org/abs/2401.03910). arXiv:2401.03910.
- Millière, R., & Buckner, C. (2024). [A Philosophical Introduction to Language Models — Part II: The Way Forward](https://arxiv.org/abs/2405.03207). arXiv:2405.03207.
- Mollo, D. C., & Millière, R. (2023). [The Vector Grounding Problem](https://arxiv.org/abs/2304.01481). arXiv:2304.01481.
- Matthias, A. (2004). [The Responsibility Gap: Ascribing Responsibility for the Actions of Learning Automata](https://doi.org/10.1007/s10676-004-3422-1). *Ethics and Information Technology*, 6, 175–183. DOI: 10.1007/s10676-004-3422-1.
- Park, K., Choe, Y. J., & Veitch, V. (2023). [The Linear Representation Hypothesis and the Geometry of Large Language Models](https://arxiv.org/abs/2311.03658). arXiv:2311.03658.
- Perez, E. et al. (2022). [Discovering Language Model Behaviors with Model-Written Evaluations](https://arxiv.org/abs/2212.09251). arXiv:2212.09251.
- Petroni, F., Rocktäschel, T., Lewis, P., Bakhtin, A., Wu, Y., Miller, A. H., & Riedel, S. (2019). [Language Models as Knowledge Bases?](https://arxiv.org/abs/1909.01066) *EMNLP 2019*. arXiv:1909.01066.
- Quine, W. V. O. (1960). *[Word and Object](https://mitpress.mit.edu/9780262670012/word-and-object/)*. MIT Press.
- McCarthy, J., & Hayes, P. J. (1969). Some Philosophical Problems from the Standpoint of Artificial Intelligence. *Machine Intelligence*, 4, 463–502.
- Metzinger, T. (2021). [Artificial Suffering: An Argument for a Global Moratorium on Synthetic Phenomenology](https://doi.org/10.1142/S270507852150003X). *Journal of Artificial Intelligence and Consciousness*, 8(1), 43–66. DOI: 10.1142/S270507852150003X.
- Nagel, T. (1974). [What Is It Like to Be a Bat?](https://doi.org/10.2307/2183914) *Philosophical Review*, 83(4), 435–450. DOI: 10.2307/2183914.
- Queloz, M. (2025). [Mechanistic Indicators of Understanding in Large Language Models](https://arxiv.org/abs/2507.08017). arXiv:2507.08017.
- Rao, A. S., & Georgeff, M. P. (1995). [BDI Agents: From Theory to Practice](https://cdn.aaai.org/ICMAS/1995/ICMAS95-042.pdf). *Proceedings of ICMAS-95*. AAAI.
- Roberts, A., Raffel, C., & Shazeer, N. (2020). [How Much Knowledge Can You Pack Into the Parameters of a Language Model?](https://arxiv.org/abs/2002.08910) *EMNLP 2020*. arXiv:2002.08910.
- Schwitzgebel, E. (2023). [The Full Rights Dilemma for A.I. Systems of Debatable Personhood](https://arxiv.org/abs/2303.17509). arXiv:2303.17509.
- Searle, J. R. (1980). [Minds, Brains, and Programs](https://doi.org/10.1017/S0140525X00005756). *Behavioral and Brain Sciences*, 3(3), 417–424. DOI: 10.1017/S0140525X00005756.
- Shanahan, M. (2022). [Talking About Large Language Models](https://arxiv.org/abs/2212.03551). arXiv:2212.03551. (*Communications of the ACM*, 67(2):68–79, 2024.)
- Shanahan, M. (2024). [Still "Talking About Large Language Models": Some Clarifications](https://arxiv.org/abs/2412.10291). arXiv:2412.10291.
- Sharma, M., Tong, M., Korbak, T., Duvenaud, D., Askell, A., Bowman, S. R., et al. (2023). [Towards Understanding Sycophancy in Language Models](https://arxiv.org/abs/2310.13548). arXiv:2310.13548.
- Shevlin, H. (2026). [Three Frameworks for AI Mentality](https://doi.org/10.3389/fpsyg.2026.1715835). *Frontiers in Psychology*, 17. DOI: 10.3389/fpsyg.2026.1715835.
- Tononi, G. (2004). [An Information Integration Theory of Consciousness](https://doi.org/10.1186/1471-2202-5-42). *BMC Neuroscience*, 5, 42. DOI: 10.1186/1471-2202-5-42.
- Turing, A. M. (1950). [Computing Machinery and Intelligence](https://doi.org/10.1093/mind/LIX.236.433). *Mind*, 59(236), 433–460. DOI: 10.1093/mind/LIX.236.433.
- Varela, F. J., Thompson, E., & Rosch, E. (1991). *[The Embodied Mind: Cognitive Science and Human Experience](https://doi.org/10.7551/mitpress/6730.001.0001)*. MIT Press. DOI: 10.7551/mitpress/6730.001.0001.
- von Wright, G. H. (1963). *[Norm and Action: A Logical Enquiry](https://archive.org/details/normactionlogica0000wrig)*. Routledge & Kegan Paul.
- Williamson, T. (2000). *[Knowledge and Its Limits](https://global.oup.com/academic/product/knowledge-and-its-limits-9780198250432)*. Oxford University Press.
- Xu, Y., Pang, W., Shi, J., Wang, X., Zhao, X., Wu, W., Chen, X., & Xu, Z. (2024). [Knowledge Conflicts for LLMs: A Survey](https://arxiv.org/abs/2403.08319). *EMNLP 2024*. arXiv:2403.08319.
- Zou, A., Phan, L., Chen, S., Campbell, J., Guo, P., Ren, R., Pan, A., Yin, R., Mazeika, M., Dombrowski, A., Goel, S., Li, N., Byun, M. J., Wang, Z., Mallen, A., Basart, S., Koyejo, S., Song, D., Fredrikson, M., Kolter, J. Z., & Hendrycks, D. (2023). [Representation Engineering: A Top-Down Approach to AI Transparency](https://arxiv.org/abs/2310.01405). arXiv:2310.01405.

### Reference Works (Stanford Encyclopedia of Philosophy)

- [Intentionality](https://plato.stanford.edu/entries/intentionality/) — SEP
- [The Chinese Room Argument](https://plato.stanford.edu/entries/chinese-room/) — SEP
- [Consciousness](https://plato.stanford.edu/entries/consciousness/) — SEP
- [The Frame Problem](https://plato.stanford.edu/entries/frame-problem/) — SEP
- [Ethics of Artificial Intelligence and Robotics](https://plato.stanford.edu/entries/ethics-ai/) — SEP
- [Sense and Reference (Frege)](https://plato.stanford.edu/entries/frege/#SenRef) — SEP

### Blog Posts & Resources

- Chalmers, D. J. (2023). [Could a Large Language Model be Conscious?](https://www.bostonreview.net/articles/could-a-large-language-model-be-conscious/) *Boston Review* (published version of the NeurIPS talk).
- Schwitzgebel, E. [The Splintered Mind](https://schwitzsplinters.blogspot.com/) — Active blog on philosophy of mind, consciousness, and AI moral status.
- Shanahan, M. [Personal page at Imperial College London](https://www.doc.ic.ac.uk/~mpsha/) — Related talks and papers on LLMs as simulation.
- Floridi, L. et al. [AI4People — An Ethical Framework for a Good AI Society](https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s11023-018-9482-5) (2018) — Foundational principles document.

---
